church.name: Salvation Temple
church.welcome: Welcome to our home! We are a living community of believers united by love for Christ.
church.heroWelcome: Experience God's presence at church this Sunday.
church.shortDesc: Evangelical Christian church serving Riga with love and faith.
about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: Get to know our church
about.intro: Salvation Temple — a place where people grow spiritually and learn to spread the Gospel.
about.vision.title: Our Vision
about.vision.text: Salvation Temple glorifies God by creating and nurturing disciples fully instructed in Christ in the Latgale region of Riga, who in turn create new disciples, leading to planting a new church in Latvia.
about.vision.textShort: Salvation Temple glorifies God by creating and nurturing disciples fully instructed in Christ in the Latgale region of Riga, who in turn create new disciples, leading to planting a new church in Latvia.
about.values.title: Our Values
about.values.christ.title: Christ is our center
about.values.christ.paragraph: Christ is our center and focus in everything we do. We remember who He was and what He did, as well as what He does now through the Holy Spirit.
about.values.bible.title: Bible is our truth
about.values.bible.paragraph: God's Word is the foundation of our faith. The Gospel and all books of the Bible guide our life and ministry.
about.values.discipleship.title: Discipleship
about.values.discipleship.paragraph: We grow through mentoring and fellowship for spiritual growth, helping each other become mature followers of Christ.
about.values.mission.title: Mission
about.values.mission.paragraph: We share the good news in word and deed, demonstrating God's love through practical service to those around us.
about.mission.title: Our Mission
about.mission.text: Salvation Temple — a place where people grow spiritually and learn to spread the Gospel.
statistics.title: Our Community
statistics.subtitle: We are a family of believers of different ages
statistics.members.hover: We are a diverse community of 150 people of all ages and walks of life. Children, youth, families, and seniors together praise God.
statistics.pastYear.title: Our church in the past year in numbers
statistics.history.title: Throughout church history
statistics.baptisms.hover: Last year 12 people were baptized. The next baptism will be on May 9, 2026. Contact the pastor if you want to prepare for baptism.
statistics.sundaySchool.hover: Every Sunday 80 children study the Bible in an engaging way. Classes are held during the service for children of different ages.
statistics.summerCamp.hover: Each summer 90 children and teenagers participate in our camps, where they experience God through games, creativity, and fellowship.
statistics.childrenGifts.hover: As part of the Christmas campaign, 800 children from needy families received gifts and learned about God's love.
statistics.familiesHelped.hover: Through the Love & Care ministry we help families in need with food, clothing, and practical support.
statistics.childrenSchool.hover: Every year we help children from needy families prepare for school by providing them with necessary supplies.
events.baptism.contactPastor: Contact the pastor if you wish to participate.
gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Our church and life around it, praising God in every action
gallery.pageSubtitle: Our church and life around it, praising God in every action
history.title: Church History
history.subtitle: Salvation Temple — the largest Baptist church in Latvia with a rich history
history.fetler.name: Viljams Fetlers
history.fetler.desc: The visionary leader of OBDS who devoted himself to evangelization, especially in Latgale, as well as missionary work among Russians, Poles, and other ethnic minorities.
history.fetler.caption: Viljams Fetlers and the Daugavpils White Church — a fruit of his Latgale mission work
history.fetler.extended: In newly independent Latvia, Fetler could preach the message of salvation everywhere — on radio, in cinemas, city parks, river piers, prisons, cemeteries, and weddings.
history.return.title: Return to Homeland
history.return.text: Viljams Fetlers returned to Latvia in 1923. Latvia had become an independent state with freedom of faith and preaching. Therefore, the message of salvation in Jesus Christ could be proclaimed everywhere.
history.obds.title: Second Union of Baptist Congregations (OBDS)
history.obds.text: After the split, the 'revival Baptists' established the Second Union of Baptist Congregations (OBDS). In Riga, the OBDS included the Matthew, Āgenskalns, Revival (Salvation Temple), and Golgotha congregations. In 1929, LBDS had 77 congregations with 6,766 members, while OBDS had 39 congregations with 3,763 members. Thanks to extensive evangelization, OBDS membership grew faster.
history.evangelization.title: Evangelization and Mission
history.evangelization.text: To proclaim the Gospel, OBDS used cinemas, community halls, market squares, and other public venues. Large-scale Bible processions were organized in Riga and Liepāja. Baptisms took place in various public locations — by the sea, on lake shores, and along riverbanks.
history.tent.caption: Above — OBDS Bible procession through the streets of Riga; below — evangelization tent in the Salvation Temple courtyard
history.temple.title: Salvation Temple
history.temple.text: In 1927, the largest Baptist church — Salvation Temple — was completed at Lāčplēša iela 117 in Riga. Many prominent guests attended the opening, including the US and British ambassadors to Latvia. The largest hall could accommodate more than 2,000 people. Services were held in four languages — Latvian, Russian, German, and Polish.
history.building.caption: Salvation Temple — the largest Baptist church in Latvia, 1927
history.temple.details: Salvation Temple had several halls, the largest of which could accommodate more than 2000 people. Services were held in four languages — Latvian, Russian, German, and Polish.
history.interior.title: Ministry in the Temple
history.interior.text: Services lasted three to six hours, but the fire of the Holy Spirit was so palpable that time flew by. In the tower of the Temple, a prayer watch operated — 24 hours a day people prayed for revival in Latvia.
history.interior.caption: The Great Hall of Salvation Temple during a service
history.evangelization.label: Evangelization
history.legacy.title: Legacy of Faith
history.legacy.text: Today, Salvation Temple continues the mission begun almost 100 years ago — to glorify God and nurture disciples of Christ. We preserve a rich spiritual heritage and look to the future with faith.
history.resources.title: Historical Resources
testimonies.title: Testimonies
testimonies.subtitle: Stories of changed lives
testimonies.testimony1.name: Maria K.
testimonies.testimony1.summary: God changed my life when I came to this church...
testimonies.testimony1.before: I lived in constant anxiety and found no meaning in life.
testimonies.testimony1.encounter: Friends invited me to a service, and I felt God's love for the first time.
testimonies.testimony1.after: Today I live with peace in my heart and serve in Sunday school.
testimonies.testimony2.name: Alexey P.
testimonies.testimony2.summary: From addiction to freedom in Christ...
testimonies.testimony2.before: Addiction was destroying my family and career.
testimonies.testimony2.encounter: The pastor prayed for me, and I experienced freedom.
testimonies.testimony2.after: For 5 years I've been free and help others find this path.
testimonies.testimony3.name: Elena S.
testimonies.testimony3.summary: Found a true family in church...
testimonies.testimony3.encounter: A home group accepted me as family.
testimonies.testimony3.after: Now I lead women's ministry and help others.

events.training.title: Training Events
events.training.content: A practical course for those who want to build a solid foundation based on biblical principles.
events.baptism.title: Water Baptism

ministries.title: Ministries
ministries.subtitle: Find your place in our community and serve with us
ministries.worship.title: Worship Services
ministries.worship.hoverInfo: Every Sunday at 11:00 we gather for worship. The service includes hymns, prayer, preaching of the Word, and fellowship. We serve in Russian and Latvian so everyone can participate fully.
ministries.sundaySchool.title: Sunday School
ministries.sundaySchool.hoverInfo: Every Sunday during the service, children study the Bible in an engaging way. Classes include stories, games, crafts, and songs. We believe spiritual education begins in childhood.
ministries.ribaClub.title: Riba Club
ministries.ribaClub.hoverInfo: Riba Club is a safe space for teens to connect, ask questions about faith and life. We organize games, discussions, trips, and camps. Our goal is to help teens find their path to God.
ministries.youth.title: Youth Ministry
ministries.youth.hoverInfo: Youth ministry brings young people together for Bible study, fellowship, and service. We hold weekly meetings, youth nights, trips, and social projects. Join us!
ministries.youngLife.title: YoungLife
ministries.youngLife.hoverInfo: YoungLife is an international program helping teens meet Jesus through friendship and fun. We meet weekly, run camps and events.
ministries.loveAndCare.title: Love & Care
ministries.loveAndCare.desc: Practical help and care for children in need
ministries.loveAndCare.hoverInfo: Love & Care provides practical help to families and children in need. We collect food, clothing, school supplies and organize support for those going through difficult times.
ministries.choir.title: Choir
ministries.choir.hoverInfo: Our choir serves through praising God with music. We sing at services, holidays, and special events. Rehearsals are weekly. All who love to sing are welcome!
ministries.smallGroups.title: Home Groups
ministries.smallGroups.hoverInfo: Home groups are small communities that gather in homes for Bible study, prayer, and fellowship. A place to find true friends and support. Groups meet in different areas of Riga.
ministries.mensMinistry.title: Men's Ministry
ministries.mensMinistry.hoverInfo: Men's ministry helps men grow spiritually, strengthen families, and build genuine brotherhood. We hold meetings, hikes, sports events, and Bible study together.
ministries.womensMinistry.title: Women's Ministry
ministries.womensMinistry.hoverInfo: Women's ministry creates a space for fellowship, prayer, and mutual support. We hold meetings, tea gatherings, Bible study, and events for women of all ages.
ministries.media.title: Media Team
ministries.media.hoverInfo: The media team ensures quality audio and visual support for services. We handle filming, editing, live streaming, and managing the church social media.
ministries.counselling.title: Counselling
ministries.counselling.hoverInfo: The counselling ministry helps people find inner healing through prayer, conversation, and biblical guidance. We accompany people in difficult moments, helping them find peace and restoration in Christ.
ministries.serve.title: Want to Serve

care.subtitle: We are here to support you in any life situation
care.prayer.title: Prayer Support
care.prayer.desc: Send us your prayer request and we will pray together with you.
care.counseling.title: Pastoral Counseling
care.counseling.desc: Pastoral counseling for those who need spiritual support and guidance.
contacts.title: Contacts

donations.title: Donations
donations.subtitle: Your donations help us serve the community and share the Good News
donations.thankYou: Thank you for your generosity and support of the church ministry!
hero.title: WHOLESOME LIFE
hero.subtitle: A practical course for those who want to build a solid foundation in an era of change, based on biblical principles and practical foundation.
philosophy.title: Course Purpose & Philosophy
philosophy.text: To train leaders and the church in biblical truths for finding inner peace and balance in the most important areas of life, as well as to connect with God for obtaining Shalom. Every person is called to find their fullness of life and dwell in peace. The result is a clear action plan for how to fulfill yourself in every important area of life according to God's design.
benefits.title: What Will You Gain?
benefits.clarity.title: Clarity of Calling
benefits.clarity.text: Stop living on autopilot. Define your mission and understand where God is calling you in this season.
benefits.core.title: Inner Core
benefits.core.text: Develop resilience to stress, burnout, and anxiety by relying on eternal truths.
benefits.wisdom.title: Practical Wisdom
benefits.wisdom.text: Get working tools: simple but powerful things that can change your life for the better.
benefits.connections.title: Deep Connections
benefits.connections.text: Find friends and mentors with whom you can be authentic and grow together.
target.title: Who Is This For?
target.text: This course is for leaders, active parishioners, and everyone who strives for a conscious life and wants to find balance (ages 18-50).
target.checklist.title: This is for you if:
target.persona.1: I have a vision but don't know how to achieve it
target.persona.2: I set the same goals but keep failing
target.persona.3: I know what I want and how to do it, but I need support
target.persona.4: I am a different person at church, work, and home
target.persona.5: I seem successful but don't know what for
target.persona.6: I'm successful in career but broken at home (or happy at home but struggling financially)
target.persona.7: I don't have my priorities straight or I'm struggling with one aspect of the balance wheel
target.persona.8: I don't want to grow but my inner voice / God says I need to change, and I postpone it, I'm not at peace
method.title: How We Learn
method.subtitle: We use Impact Based Learning method. Minimum lectures, more work on reflection, practice, discussions, and habit formation.
method.theory.title: Brief Theory
method.theory.text: Biblical truth + World theoretical knowledge adapted for our context.
method.apply.title: Theory Application
method.apply.text: You leave with a completed plan, not just an idea.
method.groups.title: Small Groups
method.groups.text: Honest feedback, prayer, and case discussion in a safe environment.
method.mentor.title: Mentoring & Practice
method.mentor.text: Assignments between modules for implementing skills in real life (family, work).
approach.title: Our Approach
approach.1.title: Awareness
approach.1.text: How does God see this? What does He want for this area?
approach.2.title: Goal
approach.2.text: Where do you want to be and what is the gap between current and desired?
approach.3.title: Skills
approach.3.text: Tools: Biblical wisdom and practical methods.
approach.4.title: Action Plan
approach.4.text: What specific steps will you take?
approach.5.title: Reinforcement
approach.5.text: Support environment and habits for long-term changes.
wheel.title: Biblical Balance
wheel.subtitle: We don't divide life into "spiritual" and "secular". Hover over an area to see its meaning in God's design.
wheel.hover: Hover over the button above to reveal meaning.
wheel.purpose.desc: Your unique calling and mission in this world.
wheel.people.desc: Relationships, family, and community around you.
wheel.body.desc: Physical health — your body as a temple of the Spirit.
wheel.mind.desc: Renewing of the mind, thinking, and personal growth.
wheel.finance.desc: Managing resources as a good steward.
wheel.character.desc: Fruit of the Spirit and habit formation.
wheel.church.desc: Your place in the Body of Christ and ministry.
wheel.piety.desc: Closeness with God, prayer, and spiritual practices.
wheel.purpose.quote: "For we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works" — Eph. 2:10
wheel.body.quote: "Your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit" — 1 Cor. 6:19
wheel.mind.quote: "Be transformed by the renewing of your mind" — Rom. 12:2
wheel.finance.quote: "Whoever is faithful in little is faithful in much" — Luke 16:10
wheel.character.quote: "The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience..." — Gal. 5:22
wheel.church.quote: "You are the body of Christ, and individually members" — 1 Cor. 12:27
wheel.piety.quote: "Draw near to God, and He will draw near to you" — James 4:8
calendar.title: Session Calendar
calendar.subtitle: Course dates from September 2026 to June 2027
sessions.title: Session Program
session.1.title: 1. Purpose
session.1.about: Program start. We will conduct a deep audit of your life and determine the gap between where you are and where God wants to see you.
session.1.tools: Balance Wheel (Biblical version)\nIkigai Framework for finding calling\nWriting "Letter from the Future"
session.2.title: 2. People & Relationships
session.2.about: We will learn to talk about difficult things without aggression and set boundaries that preserve love and respect.
session.2.tools: Non-Violent Communication (NVC)\nRelationship Audit\nScripts for setting boundaries
session.3.title: 3. Body & Health
session.3.about: Your body is a Temple. We will explore the physiology of energy, sleep, and nutrition.
session.3.tools: Sleep hygiene protocol\nCreating "Joyful Movement Menu"\nAnalysis of daily energy peaks
session.4.title: 4. Personal Growth & Career
session.4.about: Working with mindset. How do our beliefs sabotage us? We will learn to "renew the mind" (Rom 12:2).
session.4.tools: Fear Setting technique (Tim Ferriss)\nCognitive reframing of beliefs
session.5.title: 5. Character & Habits
session.5.about: Character is the sum of your habits. We will implement the science of habit formation.
session.6.title: 6. Finances & Management
session.6.about: We will move from survival stress to the joy of managing Kingdom resources.
session.7.title: 7. Church
session.7.about: How to find your place in the Body of Christ and turn your home into a place of ministry?
session.8.title: 8. Piety & Shalom
session.8.about: The art of slowing down and hearing God in a noisy world.
session.9.title: 9. Mind & Emotions
session.9.about: Working with emotional intelligence and inner harmony.
session.10.title: FINALE: Reflection & Leaders Evening
session.10.about: Final session. Time for deep reflection on the journey, reinforcing useful habits, and celebrating victories together with Leaders Evening.
session.10.tools: Year Compass annual review\nReinforcing key habits\nCelebration dinner with leaders
org.title: Organization & Timing
org.cost.value: Covered by "Salvation Temple" church\n+ 10 EUR per session (coffee and snacks)
org.commitment.value: We expect participation in every session.
schedule.title: Saturday Schedule
schedule.welcome.desc: Check-in: "One word describing your week". Coffee.
schedule.part1.desc: God's view: Reading Scripture Anchor.\n"Where are you now on a scale of 1-10?" Defining the gap.
schedule.part2.desc: Theory and Demo: Key tool through a biblical lens.
schedule.part3.desc: Deep dive into theory and practical meaning with group discussions.
schedule.part4.desc: Pen and Paper: Filling worksheets. Work in small groups (2-3 people).
schedule.checkout.desc: Homework for the month. "Your one next step?"
team.title: Team
team.elya.name: Elya Fayzulina
team.elya.desc: Will teach you to treat your body as a temple: energy, sleep, and healthy movement.
team.alex.name: Alexander Zvirid
team.alex.desc: Will reveal the topic of ministry, leadership, and creating a godly environment around yourself.
team.tanya.name: Tanya Abramova
team.tanya.desc: With deep spiritual practices of abiding in God.
team.sasha.name: Sasha Abramov
team.sasha.desc: Will guide you through the process of finding personal mission and strategic life vision.
team.richards.name: Richards Garanchs
team.richards.desc: Will help you organize your budget and build "atomic habits" for long-term growth.
team.victoria.name: Victoria Garancha
team.victoria.desc: Will teach you to overcome fears and limiting beliefs.
